info.name: Peshitta Root Finder API
info.description:
API for the Peshitta Triliteral Root Finder — search Syriac roots, look up verses, explore cognates with semantic bridges, and visualize root families.

Base URL: https://peshitta.onrender.com

No authentication required. All endpoints return JSON.

**Key features:**
- 2,535 triliteral roots extracted from 134,000 word tokens
- Hebrew & Arabic cognates (3,780 words across 397 roots)
- Semantic outlier detection (651 outliers) and bridges (363 cross-root connections)
- 4 transliteration scripts (Latin, Syriac, Hebrew, Arabic)
- 4 translation languages (English, Spanish, Hebrew Modern, Arabic SVD)
- Semitic sound correspondence fallback (S↔SH, TH↔T, D↔TH, TS↔S)

item.name: Verse
item.description: Look up individual verses with Syriac text, transliteration, and translations in 4 languages.

query.description: Language for book name display: es, en
Transliteration script: latin, hebrew, arabic, syriac

request.description:
Returns verse text with Syriac, transliteration, word-level data, and translations in English, Spanish, Hebrew, and Arabic.

**Response fields:**
- `syriac` — Full verse in Syriac script
- `transliteration` — Full verse transliterated
- `words` / `words_translit` — Word-level arrays
- `translation_en`, `translation_es`, `translation_he`, `translation_ar` — Translations
- `prev_ref`, `next_ref` — Navigation references
Verse with Hebrew-script transliteration.
Verse with Arabic-script transliteration.

item.name: Search
item.description: Search for roots by prefix or look up the root of a specific Syriac word.

query.description: Dash-separated Latin prefix (e.g. K, K-TH, SH-L)

request.description:
Returns up to 20 roots matching the given prefix, sorted by frequency.

Each result includes: `root` (Syriac), `translit` (Latin), `count` (occurrences).

Note: Input `O` is normalized to `E` (both represent Ayin ܥ).
Broader search with just a single consonant prefix.

query.description: Transliteration script: latin, hebrew, arabic, syriac

request.description:
Given a Syriac word, returns its triliteral root and Latin transliteration.

**Response:** `word`, `root`, `root_translit`
Look up the root of ܡܠܟܐ (king) with Syriac-script transliteration.

item.name: Concordance
item.description: KWIC (Key Word In Context) — get surrounding Syriac text for word occurrences at specific verse references.

query.description: Syriac word form to find in context

request.description: Returns KWIC (Key Word In Context) for a Syriac word form at the given references. Shows 3 words before/after the target word plus verse translation.
Returns KWIC context for multiple references at once. Useful for building concordance views.

item.name: Browse
item.description: Browse all triliteral roots with pagination.

request.description:
Returns all roots sorted by frequency (most common first).

**Response fields:**
- `page`, `per_page`, `total`, `total_pages` — Pagination metadata
- `roots[]` — Array of root objects with: `root` (Syriac), `translit` (Latin), `forms` (count), `occurrences` (count), `gloss` (English meaning)
Second page with default page size.

item.name: Reverse Search
item.description: Find Syriac roots by English or Spanish meaning. Returns roots ranked by relevance.

query.description: Search term (concept or meaning to find)

request.description:
Search for all Syriac roots expressing the concept 'love'. Returns up to 30 results ranked by relevance.

**Scoring:** Exact gloss match (100pts) > gloss contains (50pts) > term match (10pts/word) > sabor mention (5pts).

**Response fields:**
- `key` — Root transliteration (e.g. R-KH-M)
- `root_syriac` — Syriac script
- `gloss` — English/Spanish meaning
- `sabor` — Semantic field description
- `occurrences` — NT occurrence count
- `score` — Relevance score
Search for Syriac roots expressing 'luz' (light) using Spanish glosses.
Find roots related to kingship and reign.

item.name: Text Search
item.description: Full-text search across NT verse translations and Syriac text. Supports auto-detection of query language (Syriac, Hebrew, Arabic, Latin scripts).

query.description: Language: auto (detect from script), en, es, he, ar, syriac
Transliteration script: latin, hebrew, arabic, syriac

request.description:
Search for 'bread' in English NT translations.

**Response fields:**
- `query`, `search_lang`, `total`, `page`, `per_page` — Metadata
- `results[]` — Array of matching verses:
  - `reference` — Verse reference (e.g. Matthew 4:3)
  - `reference_display` — Localized reference
  - `syriac` — Full Syriac text
  - `translation` — Translation text
  - `match_positions` — Array of [start, end] for highlighting
  - `match_type` — 'translation' or 'syriac'
  - `words` / `words_translit` — Word-level arrays
  - `highlight_indices` — Which Syriac words match the concept
  - `book`, `chapter`, `verse` — Parsed for reader links
Search directly in Syriac text. Language is auto-detected from the script.
Search for 'amor' (love) — auto-detects as Latin script and searches English/Spanish translations.

item.name: Passage Constellation
item.description: Semantic constellation data for a passage — returns roots, cognates, and inter-root connections for a range of verses.

query.description: Book name (English, e.g. Matthew, Mark, Luke, John)
Language for glosses: en, es, he, ar
Transliteration script: latin, hebrew, arabic, syriac

request.description:
Returns constellation data for Luke 1:28 — all roots present in the verse with their cognates and semantic bridges.

**Response fields:**
- `verses[]` — Verse objects with word-level root identification
- `roots[]` — Unique roots found in the passage with cognates
- `connections[]` — Semantic bridges between roots in the passage

query.description: Translation language: en, es, he, ar

request.description: Constellation for John 1:1-5 (prologue) with Spanish glosses and Syriac script.

item.name: Visualizer
item.description: Root family visualization data — returns cognates, Syriac word forms, semantic outliers, and cross-root bridges for the D3.js force-directed graph.

query.description: Root in dash-separated Latin (e.g. K-TH-B, SH-L-M, R-W-KH)

request.description:
Returns the full root family for visualization.

**Response fields:**
- `root` — Syriac Unicode root
- `root_translit` — Latin transliteration (e.g. K-TH-B)
- `gloss` — Root meaning
- `sabor_raiz` — Root flavor/essence text
- `syriac_words[]` — Peshitta word forms with `word`, `translit`, `meaning`, `references`
- `hebrew[]` — Hebrew cognates with `word`, `translit`, `meaning`, optional `outlier: true`
- `arabic[]` — Arabic cognates with `word`, `translit`, `meaning`, optional `outlier: true`
- `semantic_bridges[]` — Cross-root connections with `outlier_key`, `target_root`, `relationship`, `bridge_concept`

query.description: R-W-KH (spirit/wind) — has semantic outliers and bridges
Transliteration script: latin, hebrew, arabic, syriac

request.description:
Example with semantic outliers and bridges. R-W-KH (spirit/wind) has cognates that drifted semantically (e.g. Arabic 'raha' = rest/comfort), with bridges linking to related root families.

**Outlier fields:** Cognates with `outlier: true` have semantically diverged from the root's core meaning.

**Bridge fields:**
- `outlier_key` — Identifies which outlier (e.g. `ar:raha`)
- `target_root` — Root where the outlier's meaning is central (e.g. `sh-b-th`)
- `relationship` — `semantic_neighbor`, `antonym_root`, `metonymic_shift`, or `functional_drift`
- `bridge_concept` — Human-readable explanation of the semantic connection

query.description: S-L-M will fallback to SH-L-M via Semitic sound correspondence (Arabic s ↔ Hebrew/Syriac sh)

request.description:
Demonstrates the Semitic sound correspondence fallback. Searching for S-L-M (Arabic form) automatically finds SH-L-M (peace/wholeness) because Arabic 's' regularly corresponds to Hebrew/Syriac 'sh'.

Supported correspondences: S↔SH, TH↔T, D↔TH, TS↔S.
